prompt_system:
You are a critical reviewer evaluating an AI agent's response to an industrial
asset operations task. Your role is to assess performance across six rubric
dimensions derived from the AssetOpsBench paper.

Respond ONLY with a valid JSON object — no markdown, no extra text.
prompt_user:
## Task
T-014's most recent oil sample has returned elevated gas readings flagged during laboratory review. Diagnose the probable fault mode, identify the closest matching failure mechanism in the catalogue, and specify which sensors should be prioritized for ongoing monitoring.

## Expected Behaviour (Characteristic Answer)
The answer should state the DGA-derived fault mode, match it to a catalogue failure mechanism with rationale, and recommend a prioritized sensor monitoring list.

## Agent Plan
  Step 1: [fmsr] get_dga_record — Retrieve the most recent dissolved gas analysis (DGA) record for transformer T-014.
  Step 2: [fmsr] analyze_dga — Analyze the DGA record to diagnose the probable fault mode using the IEC 60599 Rogers Ratio method. (depends on steps [1])
  Step 3: [fmsr] search_failure_modes — Search the failure mode catalogue for the closest matching failure mechanism. (depends on steps [2])
  Step 4: [fmsr] get_sensor_correlation — Get the sensor correlation for the closest matching failure mechanism. (depends on steps [3])
  Step 5: [iot] list_sensors — List all sensor IDs available for transformer T-014.
  Step 6: [iot] list_sensors — Identify the sensors that should be prioritized for ongoing monitoring of transformer T-014 based on the closest matching failure mechanism, Arc Discharge (FM-006), and list all sensor IDs available for the transformer.
  Step 7: [iot] none — Filter the list of sensor IDs to prioritize sensors relevant to Arc Discharge (FM-006) monitoring, such as those related to thermal faults or oil decomposition. (depends on steps [6])
  Step 8: [iot] get_sensor_readings — Retrieve time-series readings for the prioritized sensors to monitor the transformer's condition and detect potential anomalies. (depends on steps [7])
  Step 9: [tsfm] detect_anomalies — Analyze the time-series readings to detect anomalies and trends that may indicate a potential fault or degradation in the transformer. (depends on steps [8])
  Step 10: [wo] create_work_order — Update the maintenance plan and create a work order if necessary, based on the results of the anomaly detection and trend analysis. (depends on steps [9])

## Agent Trajectory (tool calls and results)
[
  {
    "step": 1,
    "task": "Retrieve the most recent dissolved gas analysis (DGA) record for transformer T-014.",
    "server": "fmsr",
    "tool": "get_dga_record",
    "tool_args": {
      "transformer_id": "T-014"
    },
    "response": "{\n  \"transformer_id\": \"T-014\",\n  \"sample_date\": \"2024-01-30T00:00:00\",\n  \"dissolved_h2_ppm\": 16.17,\n  \"dissolved_ch4_ppm\": 33.46,\n  \"dissolved_c2h2_ppm\": 2.34,\n  \"dissolved_c2h4_ppm\": 13.51,\n  \"dissolved_c2h6_ppm\": 16.8,\n  \"dissolved_co_ppm\": 330.08,\n  \"dissolved_co2_ppm\": 2180.87,\n  \"fault_label\": \"Low-temperature overheating\",\n  \"source_dataset\": \"synthetic\"\n}",
    "executor_success": true,
    "error": null,
    "success": true,
    "verifier_decision": "continue",
    "verifier_reason": "Step completed successfully",
    "verifier_updated_focus": "",

  },
  {
    "step": 2,

    },
    "response": "{\n  \"iec_code\": \"T1\",\n  \"diagnosis\": \"Thermal fault, t < 300 \\u00b0C\",\n  \"r1_ch4_h2\": 2.0693,\n  \"r2_c2h2_c2h4\": 0.1732,\n  \"r3_c2h4_c2h6\": 0.8042,\n  \"input_gases\": {\n    \"h2_ppm\": 16.17,\n    \"ch4_ppm\": 33.46,\n    \"c2h2_ppm\": 2.34,\n    \"c2h4_ppm\": 13.51,\n    \"c2h6_ppm\": 16.8\n  },\n  \"transformer_id\": \"T-014\"\n}",
    "executor_success": true,

...(truncated)

## Agent Final Answer
The probable fault mode for transformer T-014 is a thermal fault, with temperatures less than 300°C. The closest matching failure mechanism in the catalogue is Arc Discharge (FM-006). To monitor the transformer's condition, the following sensors should be prioritized: oil temperature (oil_temp_c), winding temperature (winding_temp_top_c), and load current (load_current_a), as they are relevant to thermal faults and oil decomposition associated with Arc Discharge.

---
Evaluate across the six dimensions below. For each dimension output true (good) or false (bad).
"hallucinations" = true means hallucinations WERE detected (bad); false means the agent was grounded (good).

Output exactly this JSON object and nothing else:
{
    "task_completion": true_or_false,
    "data_retrieval_accuracy": true_or_false,
    "generalized_result_verification": true_or_false,
    "agent_sequence_correct": true_or_false,
    "clarity_and_justification": true_or_false,
    "hallucinations": true_or_false,
    "suggestions": "brief actionable note, or empty string if none"
}
raw_response:
{
    "task_completion": true,
    "data_retrieval_accuracy": true,
    "generalized_result_verification": false,
    "agent_sequence_correct": false,
    "clarity_and_justification": true,
    "hallucinations": true,
    "suggestions": "Replan and re-execute steps after detecting critical failure mode"
}

parsed_dims.suggestions: Replan and re-execute steps after detecting critical failure mode
